summary:
Great! The test is now passing. The issue was that the CSV reader was using ISO-8859-1 encoding instead of UTF-8, which caused the special characters in names and city names to be corrupted. By changing the charset to UTF-8, we've fixed the issue and all the special characters are now being displayed correctly.

The needle in the haystack was the incorrect character encoding setting in the `CsvReader.java` file. This is a common issue when dealing with international text, as different systems and libraries may use different default encodings.
